concerns.text: FR-004 requires computing simple change scores (post − pre) for outcomes and then using these change scores as the dependent variable in OLS regressions (FR-005). In moderation research, using raw change scores can conflate baseline differences and violate assumptions of linear regression; the standard approach is an ANCOVA (post‑feedback outcome regressed on baseline outcome, moderator, and their interaction) or residualized change. The current design may reduce statistical power and bias the interaction estimate, undermining the validity of the claimed buffering effect.
